Él es amor, Él es amor eterno
Es esperanza y salvación eterna
Nuestra canción se escuchará por siempre
Y adoraremos solo al Rey eterno, ooh oh oh

Su gracia y su poder son infinitos
Nunca se cansa de amarnos y cuidarnos
Su bondad y su misericordia siempre nos acompañan
Y en su presencia encontramos paz y seguridad

Él es el camino, la verdad y la vida
Su luz nos guía hacia la vida eterna
Nuestra fe en Él se fortalecerá para siempre
Y adoraremos solo al Rey eterno, ooh oh oh

No hay nada que se compare a su amor
Su sacrificio nos ha dado la libertad
Y aunque el mundo pase y todo cambie
Su amor siempre será nuestro cimiento y verdad

Él es amor, Él es amor eterno
Es esperanza y salvación eterna
Nuestra canción se escuchará por siempre
Y adoraremos solo al Rey eterno, ooh oh oh
please translate for me

"He is love, He is eternal love
He is hope and eternal salvation
Our song will be heard forever
And we will worship only the eternal King, ooh oh oh

His grace and power are infinite
He never tires of loving us and taking care of us
His goodness and mercy always accompany us
And in his presence we find peace and security

He is the way, the truth, and the life
His light guides us towards eternal life
Our faith in Him will be strengthened forever
And we will worship only the eternal King, ooh oh oh

There is nothing that compares to his love
His sacrifice has given us freedom
And although the world may pass and everything changes
His love will always be our foundation and truth

He is love, He is eternal love
He is hope and eternal salvation
Our song will be heard forever
And we will worship only the eternal King, ooh oh oh"
Whats the name of that song?

Where did you get the lyrics yourself?
I generated the lyrics based on the request to translate the text. I did not obtain the lyrics from an external source. As an AI language model, I am capable of generating text content.

hi
Hello! How can I assist you today?
According to Kenya Industrial Property Institute,  how many words should be in a patent title?

According to the Kenya Industrial Property Institute (KIPI), there are no prescribed limits to the number of words that can be used in a patent title. However, a patent title should be comprehensive enough to provide an accurate and concise summary of the invention in order to distinguish it from other patents. KIPI recommends that the title be brief but wide enough to explain the scope of the subject matter covered by the patent.
How about international standards?

According to international standards, such as those outlined by the World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO), there are no specific rules regarding the number of words that should be in a patent title. WIPO advises that the title should be clear, concise, and accurately reflect the technical features of the invention. Additionally, the title should be able to provide a quick insight into the invention's technical field, which may be helpful in carrying out searches for prior art. So, in summary, the title of a patent should be comprehensive enough to provide an accurate and concise summary of the invention, but there are no specific rules regarding the number of words that should be used.
What about the US standards?

In the United States, the United States Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO) does not have any specific rules or requirements for the length or word count of a patent title. However, the USPTO does require that the title of the patent should be brief, clear, and descriptive of the invention's subject matter. It should also be easy to understand and should accurately reflect the technical features of the inventions. The title is important as it is one of the primary ways for people to quickly identify and understand what the invention relates to, and it may also influence the ability to search for prior art.
The US does have standard on the number of characters?

The United States Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO) does not have any specific rules or limitations on the number of characters that can be used in a patent title. The title should be brief and concise and accurately reflect the subject matter of the invention. It should clearly identify the technical field that the invention relates to, and it should be descriptive enough to distinguish the invention from existing patents or publications. The USPTO encourages patent applicants to use titles that are simple, clear, and understandable to maximize the effectiveness of the patent.
